articles.title: Acadeca Takes Over the State
articles.author: The Railsplitter Staff
articles.dek: On January 24, Lincoln High School's Academic Decathlon team competed in the first portion of regional competition. Academic Decathlon (ACADECA) is an academic competition in which students study tenâ¦
articles.body:
On January 24, Lincoln High School's Academic Decathlon team competed in the first portion of regional competition. Academic Decathlon (ACADECA) is an academic competition in which students study ten subjects under one overarching theme. This year's topic is the Roaring 20's, an era of jazz, economic change, and cultural rebellion.

Competitors are tested subjectively in math, social science, science, literature, music, art, and economics, and objectively through speech, interview, essay, and the Super Quiz. At our school, ACADECA is run by Magnet Coordinator Ms. Kwan and AP Seminar teacher Ms. Crabtree, who guide students from the moment the official resources are released in May all the way through competition season.

The team is divided into groups based on GPA: Honors (3.8+): Ivan Chi, Yanna Kwong, Tiffany Nguyen Scholastic (3.5â3.8): Abby Guerra, Jiali Huang, Tira Cam Varsity (3.0â3.4): Andre Chen, Yawen Liang, Jieya Liu The JV team consists of Sandra Portillo, Xinyue Huang, David Lin, Jachym Taussig.

The ACADECA competition took place at Garfield High School, where students gathered in a large room filled with competitors from other schools. Each academic test lasted 30 minutes, with approximately 50 questions per subject. Later, students faced one of the most personal parts of the competition: interviews and speeches, where judges evaluated not just knowledge, but confidence, communication, and character.

When asked about making it to state, Abby didn't hesitate. "Of course. The team has prepared rigorously and with all their might." But for Abby, state isn't just about medals. "State is going to be really fun. Last year, everyone was friends with everyone. It felt like a family. There was love all around. It brought us closer, and we kept hanging out all year."

As the team looks ahead to state competitions later this year, the coaches and students alike are excited not just for the chance to win, but for one more opportunity to learn, grow, and experience something special together.

"I have full confidence in our team securing some medals," said Scholastic member Abby Guerra. "Jiali is the second highest scorer in all of LAUSD from scrimmages alone."

articles.title: Pi DayâA Never-Ending Joy to the Math-Mind
articles.author: Jada Guevara
articles.dek: On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST Magnet planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She isâ¦
articles.body:
On March 13th, to celebrate Pi Day, MST Magnet planned a competition to see who could count the most digits in the infinite number of Pi. The winner, who counted up to 153 digits was Jiayu Li. She is a ninth grader and participated on behalf of Ms. Sara's advisory class.

She was proud to be the Pi winner, but felt she could have recited more and continued to reach a higher goal. She said, "I originally remembered 400 digits, but was stopped by the judges due to a misconception of getting a digit wrong." She prepared in many ways and one thing she mentioned was that she prepared by using a song focused on Pi. She chose to do this competition because before high school she was the president of her middle school.

She participated in her school competitions and wanted to give it a try at Lincoln. Previously she got to 150 digits and this year she passed her record by 3 digits. She hopes she will do better and go higher next year. After this event, and during lunch, the teachers and ASB set up a "pie in the face" event where many teachers participated and all the students had fun.

Pi is the special number when you divide any circle's circumference (distance around a circle) by its diameter (distance across). This number is infinite, but what makes it special is that it doesn't repeat. Pi is an infinite number, up till this day math solvers are trying to find the concluding digit of pi. Pi is crucial in everyday lives. For instance, engineering, architecture, technology, space exploration, and more use Pi. So, if you want to participate next year, just look at a circle and count 3.1415926â¦.

articles.title: The Real Life Mascot
articles.author: Sophia Miranda
articles.dek: What is orange, furry and has Tiger spirit? Be on the lookout for what people are calling our real life mascotâ¦the little tiger cat.
articles.body:
What is orange, furry and has Tiger spirit? Be on the lookout for what people are calling our real life mascotâ¦the little tiger cat.

A little orange cat has been found living on campus and taking its morning laps around the school. The staff and students take turns giving the kitty some treats. It is truly a cute site to see to brighten up your day when walking into school. So get to school bright and early to catch a glimpse of our little mascot.

articles.title: The Decline of Console Gaming
articles.author: Yew Yu
articles.dek: When it comes to gaming, Microsoft is widely considered to be one of the most well known gaming console companies in the world, having produced multiple generational consoles like the Xbox.
articles.body:
When it comes to gaming, Microsoft is widely considered to be one of the most well known gaming console companies in the world, having produced multiple generational consoles like the Xbox.

When a new CEO for Microsoft Gaming was announced in February 2026, the subjective decision to "sunset" Xbox to focus heavily on AI was revealed. Some people are not happy with this change, with some saying it is just another cash grab, while others are sad about the "end of an era".

Around 31% of Lincoln students use console gaming, while the majority games on PCs. Console gaming is definitely starting to die out as it is replaced by more modern gaming solutions.

articles.title: Are Video Games Losing Their Allure?
articles.author: Rebecca Medrano
articles.dek: It's no secret that Epic games is losing players due to their many unnecessary collaborations with Fortnite in hopes of gaining more funds off their player base. Fortnite isn't gaining users as much aâ¦
articles.body:
It's no secret that Epic games is losing players due to their many unnecessary collaborations with Fortnite in hopes of gaining more funds off their player base. Fortnite isn't gaining users as much as they usually do and with their recent choice to hike up prices for v-bucks, an in game currency, what was once originally 1,000 v-bucks has lowered to 800 v-bucks all for the same price.

People are in outrage because previously players with the Crew Pass once earned 1,000 v-bucks every month but the price of v-bucks has dropped to 800. In addition, the seasonal battle passes no longer give people the same amount of rewards. V-bucks inflation is a huge cause of many players quitting the game.

Recent updates include collaborations with bands, other video games, musicians, television shows, and much more. You'd think people would be happy with all of these collaborations but no--quite the opposite in fact! Players have been complaining about them.

Epic games isn't the only company on a downward spiral. Popular mobile game Roblox is completely destroying their game updates disguised as safety but it is now becoming controversial. Players of Roblox are in disarray with the recent "chat ban" in order to prevent grooming, however with the mandatory face scan to verify age that is so easily broken through. Pople wonder just how seriously Roblox is attempting to act.

Roblox has also made claims to remove items such as classic faces, classic clothes, r15 mode, and even completely remove stiff bodies for the sake of the more "dynamic" clothes and avatars, and people are outraged by all the changes and have protested to no avail. Roblox only bent to the whims of their community after facing a huge drop in profit.

Other gaming companies are on the rise. CAPCOM recently skyrocketed in popularity and sales due to their most recently published game Resident Evil 9 Requiem. The Resident Evil franchise is well known for their characters but beyond that the game features in depth lore and world building that most other games don't do, or put little to no effort into due to something called "second-screening".

All in all, video games aren't really losing their appeal. Players are just getting tired of the unfair changes and updates. Games that focus on fun, creativity, and listening to their community are still successful. In the end, players will stick with games that treat them fairly.

articles.title: What Annoys You?
articles.author: Samantha Ortiz
articles.dek: A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations.
articles.body:
A pet peeve refers to an annoying behavior or action that one finds irritating more than others. Some believe that the root of a pet peeve grows out of a violation of social expectations.

School can be an expansion ground for pet peeves on account of the fact that people follow different behaviors that others may not be pleased with. A student expressed that their biggest pet peeve in school is when students walk at a slow pace in the hall. They further revealed that this irritated them because such behavior "blocks their path to class".

Students also shared that the "no cellphone policy" has become rather annoying as well. One student in particular found it bothersome how administration "freaks out" about phones being out in class, especially when there are more important issues to detail with on a daily basis.

articles.title: Fake News: Can You Tell the Difference?
articles.author: Jennifer Month - Hernandez
articles.dek: Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial.
articles.body:
Today it is hard to discern real information from fake news. Fake news can lead to chaos and confusion. The ability to distinguish real information from fake news has become crucial.

Case in point is a bizarre story of a man named Ricky Hollowell who was allegedly kidnapped for 3 days by a dolphin named "Gerold " off the coast of Fort Myers beach near Sanibel Causeway Florida. Although this is an interesting story, this is also entirely fabricated information by a fakebook account page called "The dark humor report".

Another event that viralized was the alleged claim about the alleged death of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's, this case also through social media and was circulated world wide, but the Israeli government officially dismissed it as fake news. About 86% of global online citizens are exposed to false news online.

At Lincoln through a survey conducted by The Railsplitter 44% of 50 students surveyed believed that the story of Ricky Hallowell may have happened. Out of 73 students surveyed 50.7% of the students claim they check to see if the information they see on social media is real or not.

articles.title: From Practice to Performance:Tiger Cheer Team Takes First Place at Competition
articles.author: Alma Santiago and Samantha Martinez
articles.body:
On Friday, March 27, the Lincoln cheer team traveled to Las Vegas for the World Class Cheer Competition. The team had to wake up early on Saturday just to get to the event, and competed against two other teams. With close scores, our Lincoln cheer team won first place in the non-tumbling category. As their prize, they received WCC rings and had the chance to move on to the next competition. 

     Gabrielle stated, "I didn't think we were going to win because we didn't really have that much time to practice and we felt rushed. But once they announced that our team won, I was super happy and super proud of the team."

     The next competition was at 8:15 am on Saturday, April 11 in Pasadena. The team went head to head against one other team.  It was all or nothing.  Luckily for the team, they got first place once again, and received even bigger rings this time. They also won a banner saying "Two Time World Champions"  and they received WCC Crowns. Our Lincoln cheer team also won Level Two Champions in their category winning another banner to take home and "WCC Champion" t-shirts.

articles.title: Softball Surges
articles.author: Jose Beltran
articles.dek: Lincoln High Softball Surges Late, but Experiences a Tough Playoff Loss.
articles.body:
The 2026 softball season has ended and after a month of high-scoring victories, the Tigers had positioned themselves as a possible contending team in the upcoming LA City Section playoffs.  However, they lost 16-6 to the Chavez Eagles on Wednesday. The Tigers just couldn’t catch a break and have now endured four losses in a row. It's hard to turn regular season success into postseason wins, a disappointing fact Lincoln softball athletes now understand. Lincoln's defeat dropped their record to 9-8. 

   The Tigers have played with a intensity this year, but Lincoln was not able to break out of their rough patch on Wednesday as the team picked up their third straight defeat. They took a 6-3 hit to the loss column at the hands of the Bravo Knights. 

    Earlier in the year, the team won a home conference game against Franklin by a score of 10-0. On May 1, and the Tigers had an impressive week with a 9-1 win over Marshall. It wasn't just the score that was awesome; it was their pitching and hitting.  Days earlier, on April 29, Lincoln won a 5-1 victory over Bravo. The game served as a reminder that the Tigers can win against good opponents.  On April 22, the Tigers' caught fire in a 12-2 victory over Wilson, a game that showed the depth of the Lincoln lineup. 

     It wasn't entirely easy. A 7-2 loss to Eagle Rock on April 20 broke a winning streak, but they won their next three games. 

      Val Vences, a senior, has has been awesome on offense, leading the team with 21 RBIs and a batting average that has consistently hovered near .400. Gladys Barajas also a senior had 16 RBIs, often coming through in situations with runners on base.
